source.name: Chapter 1.2: 8.1 Knowledge Check - Action name() method
source.instructions: **What must an action's name() method return, and why does it matter?**

answers.answer: The filename of the action; it is only used for logging
A string that identifies the action (e.g. "action_bank_hours"); it must match the name used in the domain and in flow steps, or Rasa won't find the action
The class name of the action (e.g. ActionBankHours)
Nothing; name() is optional in Rasa

source.learningObjectives: SWBAT explain the role of the action name() method
source.guidance: The name() method must return a string that uniquely identifies the action (e.g. "action_bank_hours"). This string must match exactly: (1) the name you list under actions: in the domain, and (2) the name you use in flow steps (- action: action_bank_hours). By convention it matches the module/filename without .py. If there is a mismatch, Rasa will report that the action was not found.
